reasoning: Caruana's official FIDE rating on October 1, 2025 was 2789. For his rating to be 1% higher, he would need to reach 2816.89 (so 2817 in official ratings). Gaining 28 FIDE rating points at the elite level (where the K-factor is 10) requires scoring nearly +3 points above statistical expectation. This represents scoring immensely well against top-tier opposition or achieving near-perfect scores against slightly lower-rated Grandmasters, a feat that is exceptionally rare over a one- or two-month span. Since the November 2 and November 25 resolution dates will almost certainly reflect the November 2025 FIDE list (or December for the latter), there are simply not enough games or structural volatility for him to jump 28 points so quickly. Even by January 24, 2026, gaining and maintaining a 28-point jump is highly unlikely for a mature elite player currently residing below 2800, though the slightly longer horizon provides marginally greater structural uncertainty.
